narrative.ga_jtc_intro: The GA JTC offers a dollar-for-dollar reduction of state income tax liability when creating new jobs in certain areas. Counties are designated Tier 1 through Tier 4, with Military Zones, Less Developed Census Tracts (LDCT), and Opportunity Zones often supporting lower thresholds and larger benefits.
narrative.ga_jtc_note: Rotary Corporation Georgia locations with corresponding tier designations and estimated credit benefits are shown below. Final thresholds may be NAICS dependent and should be validated.
narrative.retraining_intro: The GA Retraining Tax Credit can provide up to 50% of eligible retraining costs, including wages of trainees, up to $1,250 per employee per year.
narrative.retraining_context: There are many software systems and equipment platforms in the logistics and distribution space that could qualify. If Rotary Corporation implemented new systems or equipment and retrained existing employees, there may be meaningful savings available.
narrative.rd_intro: Federal and Georgia R&D credits can provide tax relief on qualified technical activities, typically as a percentage of qualified research spend including eligible wages.
narrative.rd_examples_intro: Examples of how Rotary Corporation may qualify include:
narrative.costseg_intro: Rotary Corporation may be able to reduce taxes through cost segregation by accelerating depreciation on newly constructed, expanded, or purchased facilities.
narrative.costseg_detail: For operations-heavy facilities, portions such as site improvements, electrical systems, specialized power, fabrication areas, and certain interior improvements may qualify for shorter depreciation lives.
narrative.costseg_bonus: A substantial portion of commercial building basis (net of land) may be accelerated in year one, subject to current bonus depreciation rules and project facts.
